Benjamin Ravenscroft

PhD Candidate

The Wharton School,

University of Pennsylvania

About Me

I am a first-year graduate student in the Operations, Information and Decisions Department at The Wharton School. I am primarily interested in empirical and data-driven research, focussed on healthcare operations.

I completed my undergraduate studies at Queen’s University majoring in Computer Science with a specialization in Biomedical Computing. I graduated in 2023 with honors in the major. As an undergrad, I worked in the Queen’s Cancer Research Institute as a research assistant, working in both the Berman and Simpson Labs. My research primarily focussed on identifying predictive immune biomarkers for Non-Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer (NMIBC). During my undergraduate thesis, I worked on translating spatial-clustering algorithms developed for geoscience research for use in extracting interpretable features from multiplex immunofluorescence images of NMIBC that could be used as predictive biomarkers.

Under the supervision of Prof. Hossein Abouee-Mehrizi, I received my master’s degree in Management Science and Engineering from the University of Waterloo in 2024. While completing my master’s, I worked on data-driven research relating to the operations of a large pediatric outpatient rehabilitation network in Southwestern Ontario.
